text: The bond-cleaving step in cross-coupling remains poorly characterized due in part to the rich redox chemistry available to copper, and the unknown stability of high-oxidation-state copper species involved. Here we describe the oxidative addition of α-haloacetonitrile into ionic copper(I) salts and neutral copper(I) species to generate copper(III) species as active intermediates, previously unknown but fully characterized and studied. These stable species find explanation from the substantial Cu−CF 3 bonding interaction along with high barrier of the corresponding C (CF 3 )−C (CH 2 CN ) bond forming reductive elimination process. For the unknown oxidative-addition step leading to these copper(III) intermediates in the cross-coupling we identified distinct pathways leading to ionic and neutral copper(I) complexes involving an S N 2 process and an atom-transfer reaction to the halogen atom, respectively. Importantly the ligand acceleration (LAE) effect is retained for oxidative addition and is qualitatively similar in magnitude and meaning as that obtained for catalytic couplings of azoles, amines, alkynes or other activations of alkyl halides with alkyl electrophiles.
headline: Fully Human Written
